words.def: used with verbs to express movement on or to a particular place or position
words.example: Move the books onto the second shelf.

words.def: the process of cutting open a part of a person’s body in order to remove or repair a damaged part
words.example: Will I need to have an operation?

words.def: something that you can choose to have or do; the freedom to choose what you do
words.example: As I see it, we have two options…

words.def: not unusual or different in any way
words.example: an ordinary sort of day

words.def: a group of people who form a business, club, etc. together in order to achieve a particular aim
words.example: He's the president of a large international organization.

words.def: involving large numbers of people who work together to do something in a way that has been carefully planned
words.example: an organized body of workers

words.def: existing at the beginning of a particular period, process or activity
words.example: The room still has many of its original features.

words.def: used to say what is the right thing to do
words.example: They ought to apologize.

words.def: the one or ones that belong to us
words.example: Their house is very similar to ours, but ours is bigger.

words.def: used, happening or located outside rather than in a building
words.example: outdoor pursuits/recreation/activities

words.def: outside, rather than in a building
words.example: The rain prevented them from eating outdoors.

words.def: a container, usually made of paper, that holds a number of the same thing or an amount of something, ready to be sold
words.example: pack of something, a pack of cigarettes/gum

words.def: the feelings that you have in your body when you have been hurt or when you are ill
words.example: a cry of pain

words.def: causing you pain
words.example: Is your back still painful?

words.def: having skin that is very light in colour; having skin that has less colour than usual because of illness, a strong emotion, etc.
words.example: a pale complexion

words.def: the act of stopping a vehicle at a place and leaving it there for a period of time
words.example: There is no parking here between 9 a.m. and 6 p.m.

words.def: to take part in or become involved in an activity
words.example: Anyone who wishes to participate is welcome.

words.def: especially; more than usual or more than others
words.example: particularly useful/important/interesting

words.def: an official document or ticket that shows that you have the right to enter or leave a place, to travel on a bus or train, etc.
words.example: a boarding pass (= for a plane)

words.def: a very strong feeling of love, hate, anger, enthusiasm, etc.
words.example: I admire the passion and commitment shown by the players.

words.def: an official document that identifies you as a citizen of a particular country, and that you may have to show when you enter or leave a country
words.example: a valid passport

words.def: gone by in time
words.example: in past years/centuries

words.def: a way or track that is built or is made by the action of people walking
words.example: a dirt/gravel/concrete path

words.def: the regular way in which something happens or is done
words.example: changing patterns of urban life

words.def: not involving a war, violence or argument
words.example: a peaceful protest/demonstration

words.def: a small British coin and unit of money. There are 100 pence in one pound (£1).
words.example: He had a few pennies in his pocket.

words.def: used to express the cost or amount of something for each person, number used, distance travelled, etc.
words.example: Rooms cost £50 per person, per night.

words.def: completely
words.example: It's perfectly normal to feel like this.

words.def: the act of performing a play, concert or some other form of entertainment
words.example: The performance starts at seven.

words.def: used to show that you are giving your own opinion about something
words.example: Personally, I prefer the second option.

words.def: to make somebody do something by giving them good reasons for doing it
words.example: persuade somebody to do something, Try to persuade him to come.

words.def: a person who takes photographs, especially as a job
words.example: a wedding/wildlife/fashion photographer

words.def: the art, process or job of taking photographs or filming something
words.example: Her hobbies include hiking and photography.

words.def: a group of words that have a particular meaning when used together
words.example: a memorable/catchy phrase

words.def: the scientific study of matter and energy and the relationships between them, including the study of forces, heat, light, sound, electricity and the structure of atoms
words.example: a degree in physics

words.def: a tube through which liquids and gases can flow
words.example: Both hot and cold water pipes should be properly insulated.

words.def: a particular position, point or area
words.example: in a place, Keep your purse in a safe place.

words.def: the raised flat area next to the track at a train station where you get on or off the train
words.example: (British English), What platform does it go from?

words.def: fun, attractive, or giving pleasure
words.example: a pleasant evening/atmosphere/walk

words.def: a state of feeling or being happy or satisfied
words.example: His eyes lit up with pleasure.

words.def: a large amount; as much or as many as you need
words.example: plenty of eggs/money/time

words.def: a piece of writing in which the words are chosen for their sound and the images they suggest, not just for their obvious meanings. The words are arranged in separate lines, usually with a repeated rhythm, and often the lines rhyme at the end.
words.example: His collected poems were published after the war.

words.def: a person who writes poems
words.example: an internationally renowned poet

words.def: poems in general; poems as a genre of literature
words.example: lyric/epic poetry

words.def: a thing that somebody says or writes giving their opinion or stating a fact
words.example: She made several interesting points in the article.

words.def: a substance that causes death or harm if it gets into the body
words.example: Some mushrooms contain a deadly poison.

words.def: causing death or illness if swallowed or taken into the body
words.example: poisonous chemicals/fumes/plants

words.def: a plan of action agreed or chosen by a political party, a business, etc.
words.example: policy on something, the present government’s policy on education

words.def: connected with the state, government or public affairs
words.example: a monarch without political power

words.def: a person whose job involves politics, especially as an elected member of parliament, etc.
words.example: democratically elected politicians

words.def: the activities involved in getting and using power in public life, and being able to influence decisions that affect a country or a society
words.example: world/international politics

words.def: a town or city with a harbour, especially one where ships load and unload goods
words.example: a container/fishing ports

words.def: a painting, drawing or photograph of a person, especially of the head and shoulders
words.example: portrait of somebody, a portrait of his wife

words.def: something that you own or have with you at a particular time
words.example: Prisoners were allowed no personal possessions.

words.def: a thing that may happen or be true; the fact that something might happen or be true, but is not certain
words.example: Bankruptcy is a real possibility if sales don't improve.

words.def: used to say that something might exist, happen or be true, but you are not certain
words.example: It was possibly their worst performance ever.

words.def: a deep round container used for cooking things in
words.example: pots and pans

words.def: the unit of money in the UK, worth 100 pence
words.example: a ten-pound note

words.def: to make a liquid or other substance flow from a container in a continuous stream, especially by holding the container at an angle
words.example: pour something + adv./prep., Pour the sauce over the pasta.

words.def: the state of being poor
words.example: conditions of extreme/abject poverty

words.def: a dry mass of very small fine pieces or grains
words.example: milk/chilli/cocoa powder

words.def: being able to control and influence people and events
words.example: an incredibly powerful organization

words.def: connected with real situations rather than with ideas or theories
words.example: to have gained practical experience of the work

words.def: to speak to God, especially to give thanks or ask for help
words.example: They knelt down and prayed.

words.def: words that you say to God giving thanks or asking for help
words.example: to say your prayers

words.def: a statement that says what you think will happen; the act of making such a statement
words.example: The results of the experiment confirmed our predictions.

words.def: ready and able to deal with something
words.example: We'll be better prepared next time.

words.def: existing or happening now
words.example: I am not satisfied with the present situation.

words.def: a meeting at which something, especially a new product or idea, or piece of work, is shown to a group of people
words.example: presentation on/about somebody/something, The sales manager will give a presentation on the new products.
